definition:
1. to flow upon the surface, float a-top , like oil on water, (Iliad by Homer) 
2. to flow in besides, flow fresh and fresh , (Aristophanes Comicus):—;metaphorically of large bodies of men, to stream on and on , (Iliad by Homer); also, οὑπιρρέων χρόνος onward-streaming  time, i. e. the future, (Aeschulus Tragicus); ὄλβου ἐπιρρυέντος if wealth flows on and on, increases continually , (Euripides) (ML)
